name: Frontier Reasoning Models
what: Large models that plan and reason over multiple steps, powering coding assistants, research tools, and increasingly capable software agents.
how: Transformers trained on vast corpora are post-trained with reinforcement learning and tool use to plan, verify, and act over long tasks.

name: General-Purpose Humanoid Robots
what: Bipedal robots with learned manipulation aiming to handle varied physical tasks in human-built environments without task-specific reprogramming.
how: Vision-language-action models map camera input to motor commands, trained in simulation and refined on real-world teleoperation data.

name: Grid-Scale Battery Storage
what: Utility-scale battery systems that store surplus renewable generation and dispatch it on demand, stabilising increasingly renewable grids.
how: Containerised lithium-iron-phosphate cells and inverters charge when supply is high and discharge into the grid within milliseconds when needed.

name: Next-Gen Battery Materials
what: Solid-state and sodium-ion chemistries targeting higher energy density, faster charging, and lower cost than today's lithium-ion cells.
how: Solid electrolytes and abundant-element cathodes replace flammable liquids and scarce metals, improving safety and supply resilience.

name: On-device AI Processing
use: Autonomous vehicles, smart home devices, mobile health monitoring
what: Real-time data analysis without cloud connectivity.
how: Enables faster and more private processing of sensitive information.

name: AI Agents in Smart Cities
use: Urban infrastructure management, traffic flow optimization, public service enhancement
what: AI agents learn from real-time data and adapt to changing conditions.
how: Improves efficiency of city operations through intelligent automation.

name: Recursive AI Systems
what: Continuous learning and refinement of algorithms.
how: Enhances the effectiveness of AI in complex problem-solving domains.

name: Quantum Internet
what: A network of quantum computers and nodes that can transmit information securely.
how: Using entangled particles to create a secure communication channel, the quantum internet will revolutionize data security and computing.

name: Stellar Lifting
what: The process of extracting resources and energy directly from stars.
how: Using advanced technologies to harness the immense energy output of stars for use in space habitats or back on Earth.

name: Mind Uploading
what: The process of transferring a human mind into a digital environment.
how: By mapping the brain's neural connections and patterns, researchers aim to create digital copies of consciousness for virtual existence.

name: Simulation Theory
what: The hypothesis that our reality is a simulation.
how: By studying the underlying principles of quantum mechanics and computational theory, scientists may uncover evidence for or against the simulation hypothesis.

name: Zero-Point Energy
what: The theoretical energy available from the vacuum of space.
how: By harnessing zero-point energy, researchers hope to develop new forms of clean and limitless power sources.

name: Borophene and Silicene (2D Materials Beyond Graphene)
what: Borophene is a monolayer of boron atoms with exceptional electrical conductivity. Silicene exhibits quantum confinement effects.
how: These materials could enable high-speed transistors and adaptive structures.

name: Programmable Matter (Speculative — 20–100 Years)
what: Materials that can change properties based on external stimuli.
how: This technology could enable dynamic and responsive structures.
